# SM9 identity-based digital signature — official worked example (KAT)
#
# Source: GM/T 0044.5-2016 "SM9 identity-based cryptographic algorithms —
#         Part 5: Parameter definition", Annex A (数字签名算法示例).
#         Identical example appears in GM/T 0044.2-2016 Annex A.
#         Standard text: guanzhi/GM-Standards (Cryptography Standardization
#         Technical Committee of China).
#
# 256-bit BN curve, E: y^2 = x^3 + 5 over F_p.  R-ate pairing, eid=0x04, hid=0x01.
# Hash H1/H2 built on SM3 (GM/T 0004).  All hex is big-endian, left = high byte.
#
# Verified byte-exact against the standard: SM3, H1 hash-to-range, curve params,
# key-derivation chain (ks->t1->t2->dsA), l=(r-h) mod N, and S=[l]dsA
# (see scratchpad/verify_sm9_sig.py).  g and w are the group-G_T (F_p12) pairing
# intermediates, given for pairing bring-up; each is 12 F_p components (a0,1 a0,0
# a1,1 a1,0 b0,1 b0,0 b1,1 b1,0 c0,1 c0,0 c1,1 c1,0), high dimension first, per
# the standard's 1-2-4-12 tower representation.
#
# Every F_p2 value below (G2 point coords, and each of the 12 G_T components in
# g_GT/w_GT) is serialized HIGH-DIMENSION-FIRST: the "_hi" line is the
# u-coefficient, the "_lo" line the constant term, i.e. value = hi*u + lo.
# g_GT/w_GT apply this recursively over the 1-2-4-12 tower (w^2,w^1,w^0; then
# v^1,v^0; then u^1,u^0), 32 bytes per F_p component.
#
# Signature (h,S): sign uses the fixed nonce r above; verify uses (h,S).
